target.name: Sunfire
target.founder: Carl Berninghausen, Nils Aldag, Christian von Olshausen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $315M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sunfire is a Series E climate and energy technology company that has raised $315M+ to date. The valuation universe for frontier energy includes 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers at varying stages, with nearest neighbors including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $315M total-raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $441M. This multiplier reflects typical Series E-to-subsequent-stage expansion in capital-intensive energy sectors. Peer-median triangulation was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow Climate & Energy filter; the broader sector contains 19 peers but lacks the three-plus-datapoint threshold needed for reliable cross-valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $375M–$507M, with a median point of $441M. The range width reflects the limited methodological convergence available—only one quantitative method produced a reliable signal. Key uncertainties center on the appropriate stage-up multiplier for deep-tech energy ventures (capital requirements and path-to-revenue vary materially) and the representativeness of public comparables for a private, pre-exit company. Additional clarity on Sunfire's current cash position, burn rate, and milestone trajectory would narrow this range materially.
